title: update the latest version to 1.8.2
body: PR updates the latest version number to be in sync with the linked latest release https://github.com/containers/libpod/releases/latest

comments.body:
Hi @dmitris. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Can you add a signoff to your commit to kick CI? `git commit -s --amend` and re-pushing should work.

Otherwise LGTM.
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dmitris*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Of course we are about to do a new release...

> Of course we are about to do a new release...

c'est la vie 😄

> Can you add a signoff to your commit to kick CI? `git commit -s --amend` and re-pushing should work.

done, please re-approve if needed @mheon

`[Fail] Podman checkpoint [It] podman checkpoint with --leave-running `

@edsantiago This looks like a new one, no?
Very interesting failure. It looks like restore is failing as it thinks the container is running, but a `podman ps` immediately before shows it as Exited

[log link for posterity](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-5385732420009984-fcae48/artifacts/containers/libpod/5340808991735808/html/integration_test.log.html#t--podman-checkpoint-with---leave-running--1). And log excerpt:
```
Running: podman [options] --storage-driver=vfs ps --all --format={{.Status}}
Exited (143) Less than a second ago

Running: podman [options] --storage-driver=vfs container restore f999ed900f5dfbb124f54110ad3773a330e44acf0482a930c84ca548c5c2d920
Error: failed to restore container f999ed900f5dfbb124f54110ad3773a330e44acf0482a930c84ca548c5c2d920: container f999ed900f5dfbb124f54110ad3773a330e44acf0482a930c84ca548c5c2d920 is running or paused, cannot restore: container state improper
```
I mostly agree with your assessment; my one quibble is that you say "container is running", whereas the error itself includes "state improper". I don't know what "improper" means in this context. There have long been problems with `podman ps` and `Exited` vs `Stopped`; could this be one such?

Also, as you say, this is a new (to me) error. I have not seen it before.
Happy green test buttons.  Will let @mheon merge as appropriate for the pending release.
